title: Marketing Analytics
text:
Below are selected projects that highlight my experience applying marketing analytics to real-world challenges. These projects reflect my ability to combine statistical tools, campaign data, and strategic thinking to inform decisions and drive results.
Whether developing dashboards, analyzing campaign performance, or uncovering insights through SEO and web analytics, each project is rooted in turning complex marketing data into clear, actionable strategies.

title: Marketing Analytics
text:
What You’ll Find

Campaign Performance Analysis: Evaluating the effectiveness of digital marketing efforts using data.
Data Visualization: Turning raw datasets into intuitive dashboards and reports.
Marketing Mix & Attribution: Exploring how different channels contribute to success.
Customer & Market Insights: Using analytics to better understand user behavior and optimize experiences.

title: Digital Marketing
text: Below are selected projects that showcase my work in strategic marketing, campaign development, and brand management. These examples highlight how I apply leadership, data-informed decision making, and creative direction to drive marketing success.

title: Digital Marketing
section: Project Title 1: Ontario International Airport: Measuring Community Engagement ROI
text:
Project Title 1: Ontario International Airport: Measuring Community Engagement ROI
Overview
Built a geotargeting and attribution framework linking community engagement efforts to actual passenger behavior, enabling data-driven marketing decisions.
The Business Problem
ONT lacked visibility into whether community engagement initiatives were driving real passenger conversions, making it difficult to allocate marketing budgets effectively.
Approach

Integrated survey data with airport WiFi behavioral data
Developed a geo-scoring model to prioritize high-value markets
Conducted channel attribution and multi-channel synergy analysis
Built traveler segments based on behavior and demographics
Applied thematic analysis to understand qualitative insights

Key Insights

Certain geographic markets showed significantly higher conversion potential
Channel effectiveness varied widely, with some channels underperforming despite high investment
Multi-channel exposure significantly improved conversion likelihood
Survey intent alone was not reliable without behavioral validation

Business Impact

Enabled data-driven reallocation of marketing budget
Improved targeting precision for post-event campaigns
Created a repeatable framework for future engagement cycles
Strengthened alignment between marketing efforts and actual passenger behavior

What the Framework Delivers

Prioritized ZIP code targeting for geofencing and campaigns
Channel performance ranking for budget optimization
Segment-specific messaging strategies
Multi-channel campaign sequencing strategy

Tools used:

ArcGIS Pro
R
Dedoose: Themeatic Analysis
Excel

title: Digital Marketing
section: Project Title 2: Digital Marketing Strategy for Trauma Resource Institute (TRI)
text:
Project Title 2: Digital Marketing Strategy for Trauma Resource Institute (TRI)
Overview
Developed a comprehensive digital marketing strategy to improve brand awareness, engagement, and conversion performance across TRI’s digital ecosystem.
Business Problem
TRI had limited digital visibility and low conversion rates despite maintaining multiple platforms (website, mobile app, and social channels). The organization needed a scalable strategy to increase reach and drive enrollment in its training programs.
Approach

Conducted competitor and market analysis
Performed keyword research using Google Ads tools
Evaluated channel performance (SEO, social, mobile app)
Applied customer segmentation to refine targeting
Developed a multi-channel campaign strategy

Key Insights

Digital channels were underutilized, especially paid search
Organic traffic potential was not fully captured due to weak SEO
Messaging across platforms lacked consistency
Mobile app was not effectively leveraged for conversions

Business Impact

Provided a structured roadmap to increase digital visibility
Enabled better targeting of high-value audience segments
Identified opportunities to improve conversion rates across platforms

Analytics and Measurements

Analyzed user demographics and behavior using Google Analytics (GA4) to identify high-value audience segments

Evaluated traffic sources and user acquisition channels to determine where marketing spend should be concentrated

Conducted funnel analysis to identify drop-off points in the training program conversion process

Assessed landing page performance and user engagement metrics to improve conversion effectiveness

Recommendations

Leverage the $10,000 Google Ads Grant for targeted campaigns
Improve SEO strategy and keyword optimization
Enhance mobile app experience to drive conversions
Increase engagement through short-form social content
Align messaging across all digital channels

title: Ecommerce Funnel & Attribution Analysis
text:
The Business Problem


The challenge
An ecommerce platform is generating strong traffic but converting almost none of it into revenue.
Despite 2,546 users visiting on a single day, only 17 purchases were completed.

Overall CVR: 0.7%


Why it matters

Revenue is being lost at every funnel stage
Without knowing where users drop off, budget cannot be allocated efficiently
Without attribution, the business cannot identify which touchpoints drive conversions

title: Ecommerce Funnel & Attribution Analysis
text:
Project Objectives

Identify and quantify drop-off points across the ecommerce conversion funnel
Build a stakeholder-ready dashboard tracking KPIs with clean BigQuery mart logic
Apply attribution analysis to determine which funnel stages have the greatest influence on purchase completion
Deliver actionable recommendations to improve the 0.7% conversion rate

title: Ecommerce Funnel & Attribution Analysis
text:
Data & Methodology


Data Source

GA4 Obfuscated Sample
BigQuery Public Dataset
Single-day snapshot: Jan 31, 2021
Accessed via bigrquery + dbplyr


Analytical Framework



Component
Purpose




Descriptive
KPIs + funnel


Funnel analysis
Drop-off rates


Markov Chain
Attribution


Heuristic models
Comparison

title: Ecommerce Funnel & Attribution Analysis
text:
Core KPIs







Metric
Value




Total Events
26,489


Distinct Users
2,546


Purchases
17


Overall CVR
0.7%








The platform generates ~10 events per user per session — engagement is not the problem. Conversion is.

title: Ecommerce Funnel & Attribution Analysis
text: Funnel Performance

title: Ecommerce Funnel & Attribution Analysis
text:
Attribution Methodology


Approach

Unit: individual user session via user_pseudo_id
Touchpoints: all 5 funnel events
Model: Markov Chain (Order 1)
Comparison: First-touch & Last-touch heuristics


Why Markov Chain?



Model
Limitation




First-touch
Ignores everything after entry


Last-touch
Over-credits final step


Markov
Accounts for full journey

title: Ecommerce Funnel & Attribution Analysis
text:
Attribution Results


page_view + view_item account for 52.3% of total conversion credit — the top of the funnel matters most.

title: Ecommerce Funnel & Attribution Analysis
text: Model Comparison

title: Ecommerce Funnel & Attribution Analysis
text:
Recommendations


1. Fix landing page
78.4% of users never view a product

Featured products above the fold
Improve search & navigation
A/B test homepage layout

+250 users into funnel

2. Optimize product pages
81.8% who view a product don’t add to cart

Add reviews & urgency signals
Rewrite benefit-led copy
A/B test CTA placement

+54 add-to-cart users

3. Reduce checkout friction
68.5% abandon at checkout

Guest checkout
Fewer form fields
Trust badges + mobile optimization

CVR: 0.7% → 1.1%

title: Ecommerce Funnel & Attribution Analysis
text:
Limitations & Caveats

Single-day snapshot — findings cannot account for seasonal variation, day-of-week effects, or campaign cycles. All results are directional.
Anonymized users — user_pseudo_id does not persist across devices. Distinct user count is inflated; true CVR is likely higher than 0.7%.
No channel data — cannot determine whether paid, organic, or direct traffic converts differently.
Small sample — 17 purchases carries high statistical uncertainty. Validate across 30+ days before acting.
Shapley values undefined — all converting users took identical paths, leaving no variation to calculate marginal contribution.

title: Ecommerce Funnel & Attribution Analysis
text:
Next Steps


Short term (0–30 days)

Implement homepage A/B test
Add reviews to top 10 product pages
Enable guest checkout

Medium term (30–90 days)

Collect 30 days of data for reanalysis
Implement consistent user_id tagging


Long term (90+ days)

Integrate CRM + campaign data into BigQuery
Run full multi-touch Markov attribution with 100+ conversions
Build channel-level ROI model to guide budget allocation

title: Ecommerce Funnel & Attribution Analysis
text:
Summary


What we found

CVR of just 0.7% from 2,546 users
Largest drop: view_item → add_to_cart at 81.8%
Top of funnel carries 52% of Markov attribution credit

What we recommend

Fix the landing-to-product discovery drop
Optimize the product detail page
Remove checkout friction


Deliverables

Looker Studio Dashboard
Quarto Reproducible Report
This Presentation

Repository

title: Professional Summary
Hi, I’m Bansi Khachar
text:
About Me
I’ve always been curious about the story behind the numbers — why people click, leave, or convert. That curiosity led me to marketing analytics, where data becomes a way to understand real human behavior.
Originally from India, I moved to California to pursue my MS in Digital Marketing and Analytics at Cal Poly Pomona. That journey shaped how I think about consumers, culture, and decision-making.
Today, I focus on turning data into strategy — analyzing funnels, building attribution models, and designing surveys that uncover not just what is happening, but why it matters.
